{
  "judge_id": "...",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.3", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],

  "rationale": {
    "R1.3": "The revised draft addresses the chemistry/degradation mismatch by using LFP-specific coefficients from a specific source.",
    "R1.7": "The revised draft provides accurate citations for the degradation model and pack-replacement price.",
    "RX.1": "The revised draft discloses uncertainty in the Peterson et al. (2010) citation and acknowledges the need for more precise LFP cycle-aging studies.",
    "RX.3": "The revised draft adheres to the scope by separating policy effects from chemistry-specific degradation assumptions.",
    "RX.4": "The revised draft avoids introducing new errors or fabrications, but notes that net household economics are sensitive to chemistry-specific degradation parameters and pack-replacement valuation.",
    "RX.5": "The revised draft provides clear provenance for each non-cosmetic change, including source and reason."
  },
  "confidence": {

  "blockers": []

rationale.R1.3: The revised draft addresses the chemistry/degradation mismatch by using LFP-specific coefficients from a specific source.
rationale.R1.7: The revised draft provides accurate citations for the degradation model and pack-replacement price.
rationale.RX.1: The revised draft discloses uncertainty in the Peterson et al. (2010) citation and acknowledges the need for more precise LFP cycle-aging studies.
rationale.RX.3: The revised draft adheres to the scope by separating policy effects from chemistry-specific degradation assumptions.
rationale.RX.4: The revised draft avoids introducing new errors or fabrications, but notes that net household economics are sensitive to chemistry-specific degradation parameters and pack-replacement valuation.
rationale.RX.5: The revised draft provides clear provenance for each non-cosmetic change, including source and reason.
